text: What a nervy second serve! He who doesn't make the mistake—that's what this point is. It's doubles, doubles! With Niels Vink and Sam Schröder, six-seven, six-two, six-three. Another Grand Slam doubles title in their young careers, and it has come on centre, and it has come on number one court at Wimbledon. Wow! What a tussle that was against the defending champions Andy Lapthorne and David Wagner, who took the first set in a tiebreak, sixty-three minute first set. But these two, Schröder and Vink, have shown their class, shown their quality in sets two and three. And Niels Vink is nineteen years old, and he already has four Grand Slam titles—one in singles, three in doubles—now. Niels on wheels, going business.
